title: Woohoo Ive Found Something in Eva Nobodys Noticed Before
display_title: Woohoo Ive Found Something in Eva Nobodys Noticed Before
wikitext:
[]: Woohoo, I've Found Something in Eva Nobody's Noticed Before!             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Sep 21, 2007 9:12 pm I did a search on this forum and nobody's noticed it before. I did a Google search and nobody's noticed it before. Anywhere . At least, nowhere that Google picked up. So, what did I notice? I found a reference to another fantasy work within Evangelion. By accident, really. Take a look at this picture: Right, well. I recognized the meanings of most of these body parts, but I had never seen the word "farandolae". I looked it up, and it is, in fact, a fictional term. According to Wikipedia, in the book A Wind in the Door (the sequel to A Wrinkle in Time ) farandolae are things that live inside mitochondria. Take a look at the picture; "farandolae" is right under "mitochondria"! So, there you go. Another throwback. I'm puzzled that this has never been noticed, considering this episode's been out for over twelve freaking years , but hey... I actually finally noticed something that I am almost positive nobody else has noticed!

[]:             Quote Post by Morzella » Fri Sep 21, 2007 9:14 pm Well done, you get some of Misato's noodle curry soup.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Sep 21, 2007 9:24 pm so your saying that Evangelion stole the term from the 'Wrinkle in Time' series?

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Sep 21, 2007 9:25 pm Probably more like an homage.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Sep 21, 2007 9:27 pm Morzella wrote: Well done, you get some of Misato's noodle curry soup.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Sep 21, 2007 9:32 pm if they wanted to gag it, they wouldve made it a bit more clear

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Sep 21, 2007 9:34 pm Sachiel_13 wrote: if they wanted to gag it, they wouldve made it a bit more clear

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Sep 21, 2007 9:34 pm It does seem kind of sad that they put a jokoid in there for amusement purposes that nobody noticed for over twelve years.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Sep 21, 2007 9:34 pm It's like the THX Resin joke on the Longsword starfighter, or the writing below the Bumblebee pilot's seat. (Both from the game Halo). Somebody sneaks it in and forgets about it. By the way, Zeruel's symbol (chikara, strength) is also seen on the old Halo pistol, so someone on the Halo dev team must have been a big Zer fan.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Fri Sep 21, 2007 9:48 pm Doesnt that just unlock the whole damn thing ! IT MAKES SANE NOW THE STAIN IN MISATOS FRIDGE THE QUARTER SECOND CUT DISGUISED AS AS AN ANIMATION SCREWUP ! Kaji DID LEAD THE ATTACK ON THE GEOFONT ! KEEL IS A WOMAN! PENPEN IS IN FACT GOD!!!!! AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Fri Sep 21, 2007 9:56 pm MaggotMaster has hijacked DatDude's account. Somebody hit him in the groin with a beanbag before he infects anyone else!

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Sep 21, 2007 10:00 pm Well, I'm putting this in the wiki

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Fri Sep 21, 2007 10:04 pm BobBQ wrote: MaggotMaster has hijacked DatDude's account. Somebody hit him in the groin with a beanbag before he infects anyone else!

[]:             Quote Post by thedarkorb » Fri Sep 21, 2007 10:16 pm Wow thats really cool.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Sat Sep 22, 2007 7:26 am Nice find NAveryW. Some how I am not surprised there are little things in the series no one has figured out 12 years later. Probably a lot more too.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sat Sep 22, 2007 8:05 am Here's something I noticed years ago, which actually made me LOL at the time, but which I don't think has been publicized: ^ Notice anything going on here besides Misato's cross? Check this out: Ha, an empty beer can I guess the idea is that Misato's legacy consists of the cross, symbolizing her self-sacrifice to save Shinji, and... all the empty beer cans she contributed to the world!

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Sat Sep 22, 2007 8:16 am Wow, well noticed NAveryW! It's amazing that people are still finding new things in Evangelion more than 10 years after it's release! Shin-seiki wrote: Here's something I noticed years ago, which actually made me LOL at the time, but which I don't think has been publicized:

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Sep 22, 2007 10:52 am To me, the "beer can" actually looks like a rock or angel statue poking out of the sea. If it were a beer can, it'd probably have some sort of label on it, at least. THEN AGAIN, YOU'VE BECOME PRETTY RELIABLE, WHAT WITH DISCOVERING THE TREE OF LIFE IN DEATH AND ALL. /Sorry. That was kind of a low blow. EDIT: Also, remember that by the end, she had switched to coffee.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sat Sep 22, 2007 11:15 am I can't take credit for this (Reichu first drew our attention to it), but here's an example of the ridiculously subtle visual games going on in EoE. Flip the shot of her grave marker from #26'... ...and compare it to this cut from #25': @NAveryW: I can't if you serious or not, but if you are, you need to get your eyes examined. The object in question has a round rim on the end facing us, like, well you know, a beer can, and basically the same color as the brand she used to drink (Yebichu). and what's with the cheap shot over the 'ToL in Death'? When that imfamous error was pointed out on this board, I dropped it without a peep of protest, and promised to never bring it up again... Common courtesy, at the very least, would entail not gratuitously rubbing my face in it :(

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Sep 22, 2007 11:18 am I was joking, but admitted it was kind of low. That grave marker comparison is pretty amazing, though. :o Where in 25' was that?
